fleeting "rein date" with Alex Añón Ryan Pavlik Bucephalus Bikes.
thing Evanston reputation quirkiness; Alex Añón Ryan Pavlik, co-owners Bucephalus Bikes, brought that notion life morning Oct. while riding their "specialized" bikes around town during Washington Elementary School's annual "Walk-and-Roll School Day," local celebration International Walk School Day.
Bucephalus name given Alexander Great's horse.
According legend, Bucephalus exceptionally loyal horse.
Alexander special bond with pair sparked cult which expected that great leaders have favorite horse.
literal meaning Bucephalus head" from ancient Greek words, bous kephale.
2009, adopted Bucephalus' name bicycle repair shop Evanston,
Whether planning conquer world, ride around simply trying some exercise while getting places, Bucephalus Bikes shop riders depend their cycling needs.
Wednesday morning, walked house, caught sight these grown riding "horsebike" down street.
With hand full coffee other scrounging cell phone, screamed, "That's HILARIOUS.
Please stop take your picture!"
Luckily, they pulled their reins just time shots with camera phone.
Añón Pavlik mentioned they'd "galloped" along similar fashion during last Sunday's Bike Ridge event, well.

Please Canvas guides more information about change course start dates.
Make sure check that says "Users only participate course between these dates" otherwise, your changes course start date won't save.

have questions about this process, please contact Learning Technology Hub.
